_description: Wave 191: Episode 0 of the Thiccctionary cartoon explainer series.
title: Welcome to the Thicktionary

voiceover.instructions: Speak in a deadpan, dry, slightly weary documentary register. Like a 1970s public-broadcaster narrator who has seen too much. No enthusiasm. No upspeak. Even tempo.

segments.name: hugh_cold_open
segments.narration: This is the Thicktionary. A periodical of unusual girth.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary register. Slow, level, slightly weary. Like a 1970s broadcaster intro. No warmth, no upspeak.

segments.name: greatest_hits
segments.narration: Every morning, we catalogue one object of disproportionate substance. Strictly things. Never people.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary narrator. Even pace. The final two sentences are editorial directives, not jokes. Read them flat.

segments.name: eli
segments.narration: I'm Eli Hartwell. Staff Writer. I notice things.
segments.instructions: Dry, sardonic young woman in her late 20s. Observational. The audience surrogate. Reads her own intro as if she's mildly bored by the formality of it. No smile in the voice.

segments.name: bart
segments.narration: Bartholomew Pruss. Senior Cataloguer. I will not acknowledge a subject as canonical until it has been catalogued thrice.
segments.instructions: Older British-leaning male academic, formal to the point of mineral. Stern. Reads taxonomy as religious doctrine. Slight clip on the consonants. No warmth.

segments.name: teddy
segments.narration: Theodore Vance. Junior Cataloguer. The Saturn Five is, in fact, a vehicle.
segments.instructions: Earnest young man in his mid 20s. Reverent toward the catalogue. Slightly flowery cadence. Sincere enthusiasm, never sarcastic. Treats his line about the Saturn Five as a serious correction.

segments.name: margie
segments.narration: Margaret Whitmore-Hessian. Editor-in-Chief. This publication is on par with The Economist.
segments.instructions: Confident older woman with gravitas. Slight upper-class British or transatlantic affect. Believes every word she says. Reads the comparison to The Economist with complete sincerity, no irony.

segments.name: spider
segments.narration: Spider Hennessy. Field Correspondent. I file from improbable locations.
segments.instructions: Weathered middle-aged male field journalist. Slightly gravelly voice. Sounds like he's been outside in the wind for years. Atmospheric, low. Almost a whisper at the end.

segments.name: hugh_closer
segments.narration: And me. I read the scripts.
segments.instructions: Deadpan to the point of mineral. Almost no inflection. Read literally as if the line is a job description, not a joke.

segments.name: outro
segments.text:
A NEW ENTRY
EACH MORNING

thiccctionary.com
segments.narration: A new entry each morning. Thicktionary dot com.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary close. Slow falling tone on 'dot com'. Like the end of a public radio segment.
